claimText: The Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) serves as the Nodal Authority for the Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme of India (GHCI).
evidenceExcerpt: The Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) is the Nodal Authority for the Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme of India (GHCI).
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the nodal agency for green hydrogen certification.
distractorHooks: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) directly

claimText: Green Certification under the GHCI requires an average emission intensity of 2 kg CO2eq/kg H2 or less.
evidenceExcerpt: Green Certification is conditional on meeting an average emission intensity requirement of 2 kg CO2eq/kg H2 or less.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the specific emission threshold for green hydrogen.

claimText: Turquoise Hydrogen is produced through the pyrolysis of methane.
evidenceExcerpt: Turquoise Hydrogen is produced through Pyrolysis using Methane
examUse: Correct-pair question matching hydrogen color codes to their production methods.
distractorHooks: Steam methane reforming without carbon capture

claimText: Pink Hydrogen is produced using electrolysis powered by nuclear energy.
evidenceExcerpt: Pink Hydrogen uses Electrolysis powered by Nuclear Energy.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching hydrogen color codes to their energy sources.

claimText: The National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) aims to achieve a production target of 5 MMT per annum of Green Hydrogen by 2030.
evidenceExcerpt: NGHM targets the production of 5 MMT per annum of Green Hydrogen by 2030.
examUse: Statement-based question on the quantitative targets of the NGHM.

claimText: Deendayal Port, V.O. Chidambaranar Port, and Paradip Port are recognized as Green Hydrogen Hubs under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
evidenceExcerpt: Deendayal Port, V.O. Chidambaranar Port, and Paradip Port have been recognized as Green Hydrogen Hubs under NGHM.
examUse: Multiple-choice question identifying designated Green Hydrogen Hubs in India.

claimText: The production of green hydrogen is water-intensive, requiring up to 9 litres of water per kilogram of hydrogen produced.
evidenceExcerpt: Green hydrogen production requires up to 9 litres of water per kg of hydrogen and demands high electricity.
examUse: Statement-based question on the resource requirements and environmental footprint of green hydrogen production.
distractorHooks: Requires 50 litres of water per kg
